continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends late post-Endor/post-NJO era covering Dark Nest, Legacy of the Force, Fate of the Jedi, and Crucible-style late Big Three transition. Excludes the far-future Legacy comics except as future guards.
continuity.notes: Development deck. Keep separate from Disney canon and from earlier New Republic/NJO decks unless they are active in the same Saga stack.

content.fact: Kyp Durron remains a strong independent Jedi voice whose judgment may conflict with cautious Council politics.
content.injection: In late Legends, Kyp can challenge consensus without being written as a villain or reckless caricature.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Late post-Endor Legends.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.
content.notes: Generated development Lorecard pending human review.

content.fact: Corran Horn brings Jedi, pilot, and former law-enforcement instincts into late-era crises.
content.injection: When Corran appears in late Legends, combine investigator discipline, pilot instincts, and Jedi restraint; do not write him as a generic warrior or purely mystical Council voice.

content.fact: Saba Sebatyne provides a Barabel warrior-Jedi perspective that can be blunt, loyal, and tactically direct.
content.injection: In late-post-Endor scenes, write Saba Sebatyne with a distinctive Barabel/Jedi warrior perspective and Council weight; do not flatten her into a generic Jedi Master voice.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Late post-Endor Legends.

context.label: Legacy of the Force / Caedus War

continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends late post-Endor/post-NJO era covering Dark Nest, Legacy of the Force, Fate of the Jedi, and Crucible-style late Big Three transition. Excludes the far-future Legacy comics except as future guards.
continuity.notes: Development deck. Keep separate from Disney canon and from earlier New Republic/NJO decks unless they are active in the same Saga stack.

content.fact: Admiral Niathal represents Galactic Alliance military pragmatism and institutional pressure during the Caedus crisis.
content.injection: When Niathal appears, use military legitimacy, calculation, and political tension around Caedus. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Legacy of the Force / Caedus War.
Respect story-position bounds from Legacy of the Force Begins / Betrayal to Caedus Falls / Invincible.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.
content.notes: Generated development Lorecard pending human review.

content.fact: Cal Omas is tied to pre-Daala/early late-era Galactic Alliance leadership and should not be treated as permanently in charge across FOTJ.
content.injection: When active in Legacy of the Force / Caedus War, gate Cal Omas leadership state to its proper late-era window and do not overwrite Daala-era politics.
